description:
Goal: execute compiler2 macro functions as ordinary lowered/backend-interpreted Fz functions over Fz-shaped quoted AST values, with macro readiness exposed as a first-class fact and returned values rooted in the same quoted source heap.

Signal:
- a local defmacro inc(x) do quote do: unquote(x) + 1 end end shares the ordinary function lowering/body/entry-dispatch/backend substrate and runs through the backend interpreter
- macro functions receive a hidden __CALLER__ input plus quoted argument roots as ordinary Fz values; user-visible arity remains the defmacro arity
- macro return values are AnyValueRef roots in the active quoted source heap; {heap, root} remains the source identity and no AST copy/codec is used
- quote/unquote lower as source-value construction/splice operations over compiler2's quoted source schema; the fz2 path does not call frontend::macros::expand_program_with_types, exec::ast_value, or CompileTimeEvaluator
- MacroExecutable(function) is a work-graph fact over the shared artifact ladder, not a second evaluator or macro-only lowered body
- normal runtime roots reject macro entries, and macro roots stop at BackendProgram instead of entering native codegen

Change:
- add MacroExecutable(function) and BuildMacroExecutable(function); the job creates a hidden macro root with explicit Any inputs and waits on the ordinary BackendProgram(root)
- make LowerFunction and EntryDispatch shared for macros by adding the compile-time ABI slot rather than rejecting macros in the lowerer
- lower quote/unquote to existing Const/Tuple/List/Map steps that build quoted source values directly on the active process heap
- allow the backend interpreter to run a BackendProgram entry over a lent QuotedSourceHeap process and return that same process to the source root owner
- keep compile-time compiler-host publication out of this layer; it needs source-production session authority and is tracked by the follow-up host-services ticket before recursive expansion

Why:
- macros are compiled functions with a different caller/argument convention, not a second language implementation
- source identity requires running over the source heap, not copying AST values into a temporary evaluator heap
- Fz.Compiler publication must enter the .11.7.4 source publication boundary with a live source session; adding it here would either fake the authority or alias World unsafely while interpreting
title: Compiler2 macro runtime: shared lowering/IR over source heaps
